company: Max Planck Institute for Extraterrestrial Physics, Germany
description: Research Scientist (PhD Researcher in Astrophysics) – lead end-to-end data-driven projects and analysed large complex datasets (millions of entries) to discover and classify rare astrophysical events.

projects.title: X-ray survey data analysis
bullets: Developed multi-step pipelines in Python to identify rare astrophysical sources.
Documented results in a clean, publicly available catalogue consisting of hundreds of rows and columns.

bullets.label: The analysis included:
items: Bayesian inference and MCMC sampling to model complex systems using heterogeneous datasets.
Statistical modeling of source populations with corrections for bias and selection effects.
Time-series trend and anomaly detection, nonlinear regression, and parametric model fitting to predict observability of astrophysical sources.

projects.title: Exploration of a population of rare astrophysical events
bullets: Focused on identifying tidal disruption events – extremely rare high-energy phenomena where black holes tear apart stars.
Developed an efficient search methodology that doubled the known number of such events, selecting 30 true candidates out of 1.5 million sources.
The work was cited 30+ times within the first year.

projects.title: Data Operations and experimentation
bullets: Real-time data analysis: performed real-time anomaly detection on large streams of astronomical data during live satellite operations, leading to discoveries of unique sources and subsequent publications.
Research planning: successfully wrote technical scientific proposals to obtain time on competitive ground- and space-based telescopes.
Designed observational experiment strategies and led data acquisition.
Operations: coordinated and scheduled shared telescope resources for a team of scientists.
Data pipelines: transformed raw noisy data into clean, analysis-ready datasets.

description: Research Intern – developed pipelines and performed physical modeling to explore population properties and distributions of obscured quasars.
Research Intern at the Institute of Astronomy – worked with heterogeneous datasets to identify rare obscured quasars, using applied statistical modeling and signal analysis.
